title: TLS, SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, Bucket Keys, and Encryption Boundaries · Execution Trace: Transport At Rest Client
summary: A service labels default encryption end-to-end, grants a broad KMS key policy, logs encryption context, and cannot rotate or revoke access without breaking unknown consumers. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the transport at rest client increment in encryption and key-authorization contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Separate TLS in transit, S3 server-side encryption, KMS envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff.
Review effective permissions with policy analysis, simulation, denied changed cases, temporary credentials, and CloudTrail evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Transport At Rest Client · Theory Transport At Rest Client
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 5679 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate transport, storage, KMS authorization, context, and rotation evidence; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate TLS in transit, S3 server-side encryption, KMS envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Transport At Rest Client contract for the supplied data.
